ai-books.name: 📚 AI & Tech Books
ai-books.description: Essential books about artificial intelligence, machine learning, and the future of technology.

products.name: Life 3.0: Being Human in the Age of AI by Max Tegmark
products.description: NYT bestseller exploring how AI will affect crime, war, justice, jobs, society, and our sense of being human. By MIT professor Max Tegmark.

products.name: Superintelligence: Paths, Dangers, Strategies by Nick Bostrom
products.description: The seminal work on AI risk that sparked global conversation. Oxford philosopher Nick Bostrom explores paths to superintelligence and control.

products.name: The Alignment Problem: Machine Learning and Human Values by Brian Christian
products.description: A jaw-dropping exploration of everything that can go wrong when we build AI systems and the movement to fix it.

products.name: Co-Intelligence: Living and Working with AI by Ethan Mollick
products.description: Wharton professor Ethan Mollick shows how to harness AI as a co-worker, co-teacher, and coach. A practical guide to thriving with artificial intelligence.

products.name: The Coming Wave: Technology, Power, and the 21st Century's Greatest Dilemma by Mustafa Suleyman
products.description: From the co-founder of DeepMind, a stark and urgent look at the coming wave of AI and synthetic biology, and how we must contain them before they contain us.

products.name: Nexus: A Brief History of Information Networks from the Stone Age to AI by Yuval Noah Harari
products.description: From the bestselling author of Sapiens, a groundbreaking history of how information networks have shaped civilization, leading to today's AI revolution.

products.name: AI 2041: Ten Visions for Our Future by Kai-Fu Lee and Chen Qiufan
products.description: Ten gripping short stories from a celebrated sci-fi writer, paired with expert analysis from an AI pioneer, painting a vivid picture of life in 2041.

products.name: Deep Utopia: Life and Meaning in a Solved World by Nick Bostrom
products.description: Oxford philosopher Nick Bostrom tackles the ultimate question: what is the point of life if AI solves all our problems? A mind-expanding exploration.
